zh.lifeSummary: 當Sarah Jane Swafford於1769年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加北卡羅來納州時，她的父親William M Swafford23歲，母親Eleanor Peyton23歲。她於1788年10月12日與John Diffee Sr在美國北卡羅來納州結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1814年在美國田納西州亨德森享年45歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sarah Jane Swafford于1769年出生在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加北卡罗来纳州时，她的父亲William M Swafford23岁，母亲Eleanor Peyton23岁。她于1788年10月12日与John Diffee Sr在美国北卡罗来纳州结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1814年在美国田纳西州亨德森享年45岁。

nameMeanings.name: Swafford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: probably a habitational name from a lost or unidentified place. Compare   Swofford  .

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

nameMeanings.name: Jane
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

variants.name: Jayne
meanings: Elaborated spelling of   Jane  , a form popularized no doubt by the American actress  Jayne Mansfield  (  Vera Jayne Palmer ,  1933–67 ) and British ice-dancer  Jayne Torvill  (  b. 1957 ).

variants.name: Jain
meanings: Originally a feminine form of John , from the Old French form Je(h)anne. Since the 17th century it has proved the most popular of the feminine forms of John, ahead of Joan and Jean . It now also commonly occurs as the second element in combinations such as Sarah-Jane. In Britain it is still one of the most frequent of all girls' names. It is not a royal name, but was borne by the tragic Lady Jane Grey ( 1537–54 ), who was unwillingly proclaimed queen in 1553 , deposed nine days later, and executed the following year. Seventy years earlier, the name had come into prominence as that of Jane Shore , mistress of King Edward IV and subsequently of Thomas Grey , 1st Marquess of Dorset, Lady Jane's grandfather. Jane Shore's tribulations in 1483 at the hands of Richard III , Edward's brother and successor, became the subject of popular ballads and plays, which may well have increased the currency of the name in the 16th century. A 19th-century influence was its use as the name of the central character in Charlotte Brontë's novel Jane Eyre ( 1847 ). From 1932 to 1959 it was used as the name of a cheerful and scantily clad beauty whose adventures were chronicled in a strip cartoon in the Daily Mirror. It is also borne by the American film stars Jane Russell ( 1921–2011 ) and Jane Fonda ( b. 1937 ).

historicEvents.title: Edenton Tea Party
historicEvents.description: On October 25, 1774, the Edenton Tea Party took place. It was the first organized women's protest. They put their cups down and refused to buy any tea.
Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

historicEvents.title: A Free America
historicEvents.description: The Revolutionary War ended with the signing of the Treaty of Paris which gave the new nation boundries on which they could expand and trade with other countries without any problems.

historicEvents.title: Shays' Rebellion
historicEvents.description: Caused by war veteran Daniel Shays, Shays' Rebellion was to protest economic and civil rights injustices that he and other farmers were seeing after the Revolutionary War. Because of the Rebellion it opened the eyes of the governing officials that the Articles of Confederation needed a reform. The Rebellion served as a guardrail when helping reform the United States Constitution.

historicEvents.title: Creating the Eleventh Amendment
historicEvents.description: The Eleventh Amendment restricts the ability of any people to start a lawsuit against the states in federal court.

historicEvents.title: Tennessee Becomes a State
historicEvents.description: On June 1, 1796, Tennessee became the 16th state.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Tennessee Secretart of State-From Territory to State

historicEvents.description: War of 1812. U.S. declares war on Britain over British interference with American maritime shipping and westward expansion.

en.lifeSummary: When Sarah Jane Swafford was born in 1769, in North Carolina, British Colonial America, her father, William M Swafford, was 23 and her mother, Eleanor Peyton, was 23. She married John Diffee Sr on 12 October 1788, in North Carolina, United States. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. She died in 1814, in Henderson, Tennessee, United States, at the age of 45.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sarah Jane Swafford est née en 1769, à Caroline du Nord, Amérique coloniale britannique, son père, William M Swafford, avait 23 ans et sa mère, Eleanor Peyton, avait 23 ans. Elle a épousé John Diffee Sr le 12 octobre 1788, à Caroline du Nord, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée en 1814, à Henderson, Tennessee, États-Unis, à l’âge de 45 ans.

Oktober 1788 in North Carolina, Vereinigte Staaten John Diffee Sr geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb 1814 im Alter von 45 Jahren in Henderson, Tennessee, Vereinigte Staaten.

ja.lifeSummary: Sarah Jane Swaffordが1769年にイギリス植民地アメリカノースカロライナ州で生まれた時，父William M Swaffordは23歳，母Eleanor Peytonは23歳であった。1788年10月12日にアメリカ合衆国ノースカロライナ州でJohn Diffee Srと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1814年にアメリカ合衆国テネシー州ヘンダーソンで45歳で死去した。

ko.Randolph, North Carolina, British Coloni: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 노스캐롤라이나 주 랜돌프
ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Jane Swafford(이)가 1769년 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 노스캐롤라이나 주에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 William M Swafford(은)는 23세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Eleanor Peyton(은)는 23세였다. 그녀는 1788년 10월 12일 미국 노스캐롤라이나 주에서 John Diffee Sr(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1814년 미국 테네시 주 헨더슨에서 45세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.Randolph, North Carolina, British Coloni: рандолф, Северная Каролина, бритиш колоняль амирика
ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sarah Jane Swafford родилась в 1769 г. в местности Северная Каролина, бритиш колоняль амирика, её отцу по имени William M Swafford было 23 года, а матери, которую звали Eleanor Peyton, 23. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Diffee Sr 12 октября 1788 г. в местности Северная Каролина, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла в 1814 г. в местности хендирсон, Теннесси, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 45 лет.
